---
title: Pre-processing of single-cell MB tumour samples
date: 2023-05-03
metadata-files: 
  - _metadata.yml
  - ../_quarto.yml
---

## Goal of this analysis

To create a Seurat object of all the single-cell MB tumour samples from Vladoiu et al. 2019.


## Files and directories

### Code

See `<tumour Vladoiu software directory>/create_srat.R`.

### Output

The following bash command was run to generate the Seurat object (the working directory was set as the output directory).

```bash
$ Rscript /CBL_scRNAseq/software/tumour/Vladoiu/create_srat.R \
      --in_dir /isilon/CBL_scRNAseq-archived/data/tumour/Vladoiu/ \
      --out_dir ./ \
      &> create_srat.log
```

The figures are screenshots of the plots after running

```r
VlnPlot(srat_merged, c("nFeature_RNA", "nCount_RNA", "percent.mt"))
```
in the R console.


## Results

### QC pre-processing

All filtering steps were followed according to the steps outlined in Vladoiu et al. 2019.

First, a Seurat object was created for each of the samples. This included an initial filtering step:

- genes expressed in fewer than 3 cells were filtered out;
- cells expressing fewer than 200 genes were filtered out.

The QC metrics for all the samples can be found in @fig-qc-before.

![QC metrics before the second filtering step.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/qc_before_filtering.png){#fig-qc-before}

The second round of filtering removed:

- cells with a high number of counts (`nCount_RNA`, 4 standard deviations above the median);
- cells with a high percentage of mitochondrial genes (`percent.mt`, 4 standard deviations about the median).

The subsequent QC metrics can be found in @fig-qc-after.

![QC metrics after the second filtering step.](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/qc_after_filtering.png){#fig-qc-after}

### Normalization and dimensionality reduction

The counts were merged, then `SCTransform` was run on the merged Seurat object to normalize the data with cell cycle scores regressed out.

PCA was run to find the top 100 PCs. The elbow plot is shown in @fig-pca.

::: {#fig-pca}

![](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/pca_elbow.png)

![](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/pca.png)

PCA figures. (a) Elbow plot of the top 100 PCs. (b) Plot showing the top 2 PCs coloured by sample (left) and cluster (right).

:::
  
UMAP was run using the top 50 PCs.

![UMAP of the cells coloured by sample (left) and cluster (right).](/CBL_scRNAseq/results/tumour/Vladoiu/20230503/umap.png){#fig-umap}
